schemes.label: ECDSA-STARK (baseline)
schemes.notes: Classical control. Shor-breakable; not a PQ candidate. Proves the pipeline.

schemes.label: Falcon-512 (FN-DSA)
schemes.standardized: draft (FIPS 206); hash-to-point XOF swapped to BLAKE2s (non-standard)
schemes.offchain_prep: signer supplies mul hint (s1*h mod q; +29 sig felts)
schemes.notes: Smallest sig/key; demonstrated on Starknet (s2morrow). Hint-based verify (2 forward NTTs); BLAKE2s XOF keeps hash-to-point on-chain (message-binding) at corelib cost. Fragile signing; Cat-1.

schemes.label: Falcon-512 direct (no hint)
schemes.standardized: draft (FIPS 206); hash-to-point XOF swapped to BLAKE2s (non-standard)
schemes.notes: Textbook verification equation: s1*h computed on-chain as INTT(NTT(s1) . h_ntt) — 2 transforms because the pk is stored NTT-domain (a coefficient-domain pk would need a third). Half the signature calldata of the hint variant; no signer-supplied hint in the trust surface.

schemes.label: Falcon-512 SHAKE-256 (standard)
schemes.offchain_prep: signer supplies mul hint (s1*h mod q; +29 sig felts)
schemes.notes: Standards-compliant hash-to-point: SHAKE-256 exactly per the Falcon spec, so signatures interoperate with any compliant signer (e.g. falcon.py) without a custom XOF. Same hint-based verify as falcon_512. On-chain cost is dominated by a pure-Cairo Keccak-f[1600] (flat unrolled rounds, lazy squeeze): the bare verifier uses 4.16x the gas and 3.20x the steps of the native-Poseidon variant, while remaining within both validation caps. A native keccak_f1600 syscall (SNIP-32) would close the remaining gap.

schemes.label: Falcon-512 SHAKE-256 direct (standard, no hint)
schemes.notes: The closest on-chain match to a bare standard Falcon signature: standard SHAKE-256 hash-to-point with the hint-free direct core (s1*h computed on-chain), so only the 31-felt s1||salt is supplied and the input trust surface is minimal. The FIPS signature carries no hint, so this variant mirrors it most faithfully. The most expensive Falcon variant here (SHAKE hash over the direct core), still within both validation caps.

schemes.label: Falcon-512 Poseidon (native)
schemes.offchain_prep: signer supplies mul hint (s1*h mod q; +29 sig felts)
schemes.notes: Message-binding hash-to-point built on the native Poseidon builtin (hades_permutation squeeze), reusing the falcon_512 hint verify. The cheapest variant on-chain, marginally below BLAKE2s; the standard SHAKE-256 bare verifier uses 4.16x its gas and 3.20x its steps. It fits the validation cap with wide margin, and native field arithmetic makes it the most STARK-proving-friendly hash-to-point. Non-standard, so it needs a matching signer (a vendored Poseidon permutation in gen_falcon_fixture.py, checked against the corelib KAT).

schemes.label: ML-DSA-44 (Dilithium)
schemes.notes: Finalized; robust signing. Expected SHAKE-dominated cost in Cairo.

schemes.label: Poseidon-WOTS+ (hash-based)
schemes.notes: Cheapest Cairo compute; needs research-grade security analysis. Large signature.
